skill.name: 3d-web-experience
skill.description: Guides practical 3D web experiences using Three.js, React Three Fiber, Spline, WebGL, and optimized 3D assets.
skill.summary: Build performant interactive 3D web scenes, product views, and scroll-driven experiences.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows these are markdown fences, prose, placeholder URLs, and documentation examples rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious automation was found. The remaining concerns are low-risk guidance to run GLTF optimization commands and a placeholder external Spline scene URL.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for hidden code execution: the reported backtick matches are markdown fences around examples. The only real shell commands are npm install -g @gltf-transform/cli and gltf-transform optimize, shown as user-run documentation with no automatic execution or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is markdown documentation, and the reported ranges align with fenced examples. The only executable-looking commands are explicit GLTF optimization examples for a legitimate 3D workflow.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Remote Spline Scene URL
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for a network indicator, but low severity: the JSX example loads a placeholder scene from prod.spline.design. No secrets or local data are sent, and the URL is part of a normal Spline embed pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URL is directly visible in a JSX example. It is a normal third-party asset load, not evidence of exfiltration or malicious command and control.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: the flagged lines contain ordinary markdown text, words such as description, design, or Desktop, and related skill names. No cryptographic API, weak algorithm, hashing routine, or encryption workflow appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic code or instructions at the flagged locations. The matches are caused by substrings inside normal prose and example text.

content.value_statement: 3D web projects can become slow, hard to load, and difficult to plan. This skill guides practical stack choices, asset preparation, and interaction patterns for web-based 3D experiences.
actual_capabilities: Compares Spline, React Three Fiber, vanilla Three.js, and Babylon.js for project fit.
Explains GLB and GLTF model preparation, compression, and loading workflows.
Provides React Three Fiber patterns for scenes, loaders, controls, and model rendering.
Guides scroll-driven 3D interactions using camera movement, model rotation, and reveal effects.
Identifies performance, loading, and mobile fallback concerns for 3D web pages.
limitations: It does not provide a complete runnable project scaffold by itself.
It does not verify browser performance or model quality automatically.
Example URLs and model paths are placeholders that must be replaced.
Some examples assume existing frontend tooling and installed dependencies.

use_cases.title: Plan a 3D Website Stack
use_cases.description: Compare 3D tools and select a practical implementation path before development begins.

use_cases.title: Prepare Product Models for the Web
use_cases.description: Create a workflow for exporting, compressing, loading, and testing product models.

use_cases.title: Improve an Immersive Landing Page
use_cases.description: Add scroll-based 3D motion while keeping loading, mobile support, and usability in scope.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a 3D Stack
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new 3D web feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between Spline, React Three Fiber, Three.js, and Babylon.js for a 3D web project. Ask about my app, team skills, interaction needs, and performance goals.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Model Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate asset preparation for web delivery.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a web-ready 3D model pipeline for my assets. Include export format, compression, texture handling, file size targets, loading states, and mobile fallback guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Design a React Three Fiber Scene
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate implementation planning for a React app.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a React Three Fiber scene for my product page. Include scene structure, controls, lights, model loading, Suspense behavior, and performance safeguards.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a 3D Experience
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing immersive page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current 3D web experience for usability, performance, accessibility, loading behavior, mobile support, and whether the 3D interaction supports the business goal.

output_examples.input: I need a 3D hero for a product launch page.
output_examples.output: A stack recommendation, a scene plan, model size targets, loading behavior, and mobile fallback requirements.

output_examples.input: My GLB model is too slow to load on mobile.
output_examples.output: An optimization checklist covering polygon count, texture baking, compression, lazy loading, and static fallback options.

output_examples.input: I want a scroll-driven 3D portfolio section.
output_examples.output: A motion plan that maps scroll progress to camera movement, model rotation, reveal timing, and performance limits.

best_practices: Start with the user outcome before choosing a 3D library or interaction style.
Compress models, reduce texture weight, and test loading on real mobile devices.
Provide clear loading states and static fallbacks for slow or unsupported devices.
anti_patterns: Adding decorative 3D that does not improve comprehension, conversion, or interaction.
Shipping large uncompressed models without loading feedback or fallback content.
Building desktop-only scenes that fail on touch input or low-power devices.

faq.question: What type of projects is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for 3D websites, product configurators, immersive portfolios, WebGL scenes, and React Three Fiber planning.

faq.question: Does it create production-ready code automatically?
faq.answer: It provides implementation patterns and planning guidance. Final code still needs project-specific integration and testing.

faq.question: Can it help choose between Spline and Three.js?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares speed, control, team skill level, interaction needs, and performance requirements.

faq.question: Does it cover 3D model optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains GLB and GLTF usage, compression, texture reduction, file size goals, and loading checks.

faq.question: Is it suitable for mobile 3D experiences?
faq.answer: Yes, but it emphasizes reduced quality settings, fallbacks, real device testing, and avoiding desktop-only designs.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
